title: Friedan Chiral Index Is The Chern Number Of The Spectral Bundle Boundary
summary: Friedan identifies the lattice chiral index with the Chern number of the spectral bundle W over the boundary of the punctured Brillouin zone; because that boundary bounds a compact manifold, cobordism invariance forces the total chiral index to vanish.
assumptions: Zero energy occurs only at isolated Fermi points so the punctured Brillouin zone has a boundary made of small surrounding spheres.
The occupied spectral subspaces form a finite-rank bundle W over the punctured Brillouin zone.
regime: Characteristic-class formulation of the lattice no-go theorem.
scope: Friedan's Chern-character and cobordism interpretation of the total lattice chiral index.

source_anchors.notes: The low-energy chiral index is the total charge of Friedan's chiral current at p^0=0.
The current is reinterpreted as the top Chern character of the spectral bundle W, and the theorem becomes cobordism invariance of that Chern number.

representation: Characteristic-class proof fragment turning the lattice chiral index into a boundary Chern number that must vanish.

math_expressions.notes: The lattice chiral index is the total low-energy chiral charge.

math_expressions.latex: D = S(\mathrm d - \mathrm dS), \qquad \Omega = S(\mathrm dS\,\mathrm dS)
math_expressions.notes: Natural covariant derivative and curvature on the spectral bundle W determined by the projector S.
The boundary Chern number of the spectral bundle vanishes because \partial M bounds the punctured Brillouin-zone manifold M.

symbols.meaning: total chiral index in charge sector Q
spectral bundle formed by the range of S(0,p) over the punctured Brillouin zone
natural covariant derivative on the spectral bundle
curvature two-form of the spectral bundle

step_justification: At zero energy the current measures the net right-minus-left chiral content of the low-energy lattice spectrum.
Removing small balls around the isolated Fermi points leaves a manifold M with boundary \partial M equal to the union of enclosing spheres.
The current is the dual of the top Chern character of the spectral bundle W on M, so the total chiral index becomes a boundary Chern number.
Cobordism invariance of the Chern number forces any such boundary contribution to vanish, yielding the lattice no-go theorem.
retrieval_hints: Use when an answer must explain why Friedan's proof is stronger than a bare Stokes sketch: it identifies the theorem as a characteristic-class and cobordism statement.
